# March 15, 2026

*at 8 PM, PST*



<br>



Hello again. I feel a bit better since my last post here 6 days ago, and I'm sorry that I felt something so angry had to be the inaugural post for what should be a happier overall place for me. I'll try and keep the doom and gloom to a minimum from now on.



Anyways, today was pretty fun for me! I took my motorcycle out on Historic Rte 395 here in Southern California, which I've already done a few times in the past. This time, I went as far as the Lilac bridge, pictured below:



<img src="data:image/webp;base64," style="width:40cqi; margin:auto; display:block">



After crossing it, I headed for Valley Center, taking W Lilac Rd to... W Lilac Rd... and then turning left on, you guessed it, Lilac Rd. *I guess they really like to name shit "Lilac" out there*. Anyways, I later turned left on Couser Canyon Rd and took that to Pala, from which I took Rice Canyon back to Rainbow, and back to Temecula from there via the 395. Was a fun couple of hours despite the hot SoCal sun. It's really quite beautiful out there, and I hope to record a video of that route at some point, now that I've been through it at least once.



While you're here, I just wanna say that I really do live for the beauty of riding and discovering these hidden gems relatively close to home. If you're reading this, and you find yourself bored on the weekends, just take a drive. Do it, even if you do so alone. You'll feel tired afterwards, maybe hungry, maybe sweaty, maybe scorched by the sun, maybe chilled to the bone, but it's better than sitting at home and doom scrolling. I assure you. If there's wisdom I could impart on 19 y/o me, it would be exactly this, because he stayed home quite a bit.



<br>



Switching gears, I figure I'll take this time to acknowledge a somewhat recent musical interest of mine: Linkin Park. I grew up with their music, and recall hearing about Chester Bennington's apparent suicide in 2017. I stayed up a few nights ago listening to this on repeat:



<div class="flex-horiz flex-align-center flex-justify-center" style="margin:auto; text-align:center; gap:3cqi">

<iframe style="display:block; width:40cqi; aspect-ratio:16/9" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/XtRhhlY53p0" title="Leave Out All The Rest (Official Video) - Linkin Park" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

<div style="font-size: 2cqi">🎵</div>

<div>

*Forgetting- all the hurt inside you've learned to hide so well.*

*Pretending- someone else can come and save me from myself.*

*I can't be who you are.*

*I can't be who you are.*

</div>

<div style="font-size: 2cqi">🎵</div>

</div>



I've embedded the original music video here instead of just the song, because I remember being utterly mesmerized that it took place on a spaceship of some sort. I was obsessed with space as a kid, and would fairly often watch a show called "How the Universe Works" to get my fix. I guess that's a topic for another time, don't want to ramble.



At any rate, I recall feeling a bit melancholy listening to this song. I watched a few live performances on YT, including one from 2017 not too long before Chester's passing. He interacted with the audience like a close friend - holding hands and singing with those lucky enough to be at the front. Bittersweet was his voice, which sounded as aged and tired as it did emotional and moving. The whole performance was such a beautiful thing, and I feel slightly depressed just writing about it. We miss you, Chester. I hope Heaven feels like somewhere you belong.



Is it weird to feel a deep relation to the lyrics above? I don't struggle with self-hate, I have loving family and friends, and things are generally going well, at least in my small corner of life. It sucks sleeping alone at night, but that seems like such a minor complaint. Regardless, I still find myself so choked up, sometimes, reading these lyrics about pain and struggle. Every time I do, I have this part of me that insists it's unwarranted, and that I have no right to be unhappy given how much worse things could be going. I guess this behavior is unhealthy, and writing about it here won't fix it. Maybe I'm just more emotional at night? Who knows.



<br>



I've only now realized that I haven't stated a clear purpose for this post. You now know where I travelled today, what I saw, and what I listened to while writing this. Maybe this is all my blog needs to be: just an outlet for things I'd tell my real life friends if I didn't fear them getting quite tired of my voice in the process 😆



Maybe I'll write tomorrow, or maybe not, given how mentally drained I am after work most days. If you don't hear from me soon, please take care and have a good week!
